name: ActiveJ Codegen
summary: Productivity layer over ObjectWeb ASM for runtime JVM bytecode generation. Replaces ASM's verbose visitor API with an expression-based fluent DSL modelled on a Lisp-like AST. Auto stack-frame accounting, type inference, and verifier-correctness; supports records, control flow, and relational-algebra-shaped operations.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
ActiveJ Codegen (SoftIndex Lab) is a productivity layer over **ObjectWeb ASM** for runtime bytecode generation on the JVM. ASM is the canonical low-level bytecode-manipulation library, but its visitor-based API (`MethodVisitor.visitInsn`, `MethodVisitor.visitVarInsn`, manual stack-frame accounting) is verbose and error-prone for anything beyond simple class transformation.

Codegen replaces this with an **expression-based fluent DSL** modelled on a Lisp-like AST: `call(staticField(System.class, "out"), "println", value("Hello world"))` produces the bytecode for `System.out.println("Hello world")`, with stack-frame accounting, type inference, and verifier-correctness handled automatically.

sections.title: Expression vocabulary
sections.content:
The expression vocabulary covers:

- Arithmetic (`add`, `sub`, `mul`, `div`, `mod`, `neg`), bitwise, and comparison ops with automatic type promotion.
- Field/method access (`property`, `call`, `staticCall`, `constructor`).
- Control flow (`ifThenElse`, `loop`, `forEach`, `switch`, `tryCatch`).
- Local variables (`let`, `set`).
- Relational-algebra-shaped operations on iterables (filter, map, reduce, group-by, sort) — useful for query-engine codegen.
- Records (heterogeneous data containers) — the Java-21 `record` keyword as a Codegen primitive.

sections.title: Design implication
sections.content:
The Codegen design "drops the floor of should-I-codegen" for managed-runtime applications: if generating bytecode at runtime is no harder than writing a fluent expression tree, more applications generate code than would tolerate ASM's visitor verbosity. ActiveJ uses Codegen pervasively for its Specializer (instance-to-static-class rewriting), serialisation, query engine, and dependency injection container.

For a language designer working on the JVM (Kotlin Multiplatform, JVM-targeted DSLs), the lesson is that runtime codegen ergonomics matter as much as ASM bytecode-level capability. A high-level expression DSL on top of ASM is one of the cheapest improvements available.
